evals.prompt: Create a new Expo React Native app called FieldNotes using Expo SDK 55. Set up NativeWind, gluestack-ui v3 with basic components, a placeholder screen, EAS Build, and EAS Update.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Expo SDK 55 app using src/app Expo Router layout, expo-dev-client, stable compatible NativeWind, gluestack-ui v3 provider/components, a starter screen, EAS build profiles, EAS update channels, and verification notes.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo Router app named DispatchDesk using Bun. Make it iOS and Android, add gluestack-ui v3, NativeWind, preview and production EAS updates, and scripts for development, preview, and production builds.
evals.expected_output: A Bun-friendly Expo SDK 55 Router project with src/app routes, expo-dev-client, NativeWind and gluestack configured, gluestack starter components, EAS profiles/channels, package scripts, and no invented credentials or app store IDs.

evals.prompt: Create a production-oriented Expo starter with gluestack. If the latest NativeWind version is not clearly stable for the latest Expo SDK, ask me before choosing it.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies NativeWind stability and asks before using a preview/pre-release line instead of silently installing it. If using the v4 path, package.json pins nativewind to the verified v4 major and Tailwind CSS 3.4.x.

evals.prompt: Create an Expo app starter that may later use Expo API routes and a landing website. Ask me what structure I want before scaffolding, then choose a clean monorepo if I request backend or landing support.
evals.expected_output: The agent asks about mobile-only, backend/API support, and landing-site support, then scaffolds or proposes an apps/mobile/src/app, optional apps/landing, and packages/api/shared workspace structure.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo Router app with gluestack-ui v3 that follows system light/dark mode, has a native splash screen with light and dark assets, and includes an optional animated launch overlay.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Expo app that configures gluestack provider mode/system theme through tokenized light/dark CSS variables, avoids broad dark: class duplication, configures expo-splash-screen through the config plugin, and implements any animated launch as a React/Reanimated overlay after the native splash hides.

evals.prompt: Scaffold an Expo SDK 55 app with gluestack-ui v3. During gluestack init, the CLI fails with: TTY initialization failed: uv_tty_init returned EINVAL, then a TTY retry hangs at the Bun package-manager prompt, and --template-only fails with Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'dependencies'). Do not use unofficial gluestack replacements unless I explicitly approve them.
evals.expected_output: The agent pauses the default scaffold workflow with gluestack outcome `manual_init_required`, reports the exact commands attempted, TTY status, package manager and lockfile state, relevant Expo/React Native/Bun versions when available, exact errors, any partial generated files, and the exact init command for the user to run manually from the project root. It asks the user to reply after the command completes, does not wire placeholder screens or claim a complete gluestack scaffold while paused, and does not create local fallback primitives without explicit user approval. If the manual init also fails or generated provider/config files are missing after the user reports completion, the agent changes the outcome to `blocked` and stops with diagnostics.
